public int reverseBits(int n) {
    int result = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
        result = (result << 1) | (n & 1);

    return result;
}

For the follow-up question, if the function is called many times, how to optimize? So the code for the starter code would be:

class Solution {
    public int reverseBits(int n) {
        int result = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
            result = (result << 1) | (n & 1);
            n = n >>> 1;
        }
        return result;
    }
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int reverseBits(int n) {
        int result = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
            result = (result << 1) | (n & 1);
            n = n >>> 1;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm reverses bits by iterating through all 32 bits of the integer. For each bit, it shifts the result left and adds the current bit, then shifts the input right using unsigned shift to avoid sign extension. This approach ensures correct handling of both signed and unsigned representations. Strengths include simplicity and fixed-time execution. Weaknesses include no optimization for repeated calls.
